message:
docs(wizard): add clarity for common user confusion points

Based on user testing with a 75-year-old user who got confused:

1. preflight-check: Add prominent Windows error explanation
   - Explains 'bash' is not recognized / Get-Date errors
   - Tells users they're running commands locally, not on VPS
   - Adds this as first troubleshooting item

2. reconnect-ubuntu: Add SSH key vs password explanation
   - Explains why "permission denied" happens
   - Clarifies ubuntu user uses SSH key, not root password
   - Provides fallback to reconnect as root

3. launch-onboarding: Add detailed Claude OAuth flow
   - Step-by-step: URL → browser → code → paste back
   - Explicitly explains "Paste this into Claude Code" means terminal

These changes address the core mental model gap: users don't understand
the two-computer concept (local Windows vs remote VPS) and get confused
about where to run commands and how authentication flows work.

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -166,13 +166,27 @@ export default function LaunchOnboardingPage() {
1
-
+

Claude Code

+ {/* Detailed OAuth flow explanation */} +
+

How the authentication works:

+
    +
  1. The terminal shows a URL like https://claude.ai/oauth/...
    2. +
  2. Copy that URL and paste it into your web browser (on your laptop)
    3. +
  3. Log in to Claude in your browser
    4. +
  4. The browser shows a code (like "ABCD-1234")
    5. +
  5. Copy that code and paste it back into your terminal window (the same one running Claude)
    6. +
+

+ When your browser says "Paste this into Claude Code" — that means paste the code into the terminal window where you typed claude. +

+

@@ -25,6 +25,16 @@ const PREFLIGHT_COMMAND = "curl -fsSL \"https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Dicklesworthstone/agentic_coding_flywheel_setup/main/scripts/preflight.sh?$(date +%s)\" | bash"; const TROUBLESHOOTING = [ + { + title: "'bash' is not recognized / Get-Date error (Windows)", + fixes: [ + "You're running this on your Windows computer, not on the VPS!", + "First, connect to your VPS with: ssh root@YOUR_VPS_IP", + "Wait until you see 'root@vps:~#' or similar", + "THEN paste the preflight command", + "The preflight command only works on the Linux VPS, not on Windows", + ], + }, { title: "APT is locked by another process", fixes: [ @@ -122,6 +132,23 @@ export default function PreflightCheckPage() { Warnings are okay — you can still continue. + {/* Windows-specific warning - CRITICAL for confused users */} + +
+

+ If you paste this command and see errors like 'bash' is not recognized or + Get-Date : Cannot bind parameter: +

+

+ You're running this on your Windows computer, NOT on the VPS! +

+

+ Go back to your terminal, type ssh root@{displayIP}, + enter your VPS password, and THEN paste the preflight command. +

+
+ + {/* Command */}

Run this command

@@ -154,6 +154,32 @@ export default function ReconnectUbuntuPage() { showCheckbox persistKey="reconnect-ubuntu" /> + + {/* Common mistake: using wrong credentials */} + +
+

This means one of two things:

+
    +
  1. + SSH key wasn't set up correctly — + the installer needs to complete successfully for this to work +
    2. +
  2. + You're using the wrong credentials — + the ubuntu user uses your SSH key, NOT the root password +
    3. +
+

+ If you're being asked for a password, try connecting as root instead: +

+ + ssh root@{vpsIP} + +

+ Use the VPS root password (the one from your provider), then re-run the installer. +

+
+
